reasoning:
The threshold is fixed at 168.5 events per 30 days (the average for the preceding 360 days). Forecasting whether future 30-day periods will exceed this amount depends strongly on seasonality and trend. Conflict in Nigeria (Boko Haram, farmer-herder clashes, banditry) exhibits pronounced seasonality: violence peaks during the dry season (approx. November through May, particularly Q1) due to unimpeded mobility, and falls during the wet season (June through October, especially August/September) when roads wash out and movement is restricted. 

1) 2025-03-23 (Feb 21-Mar 23): Strongly in the dry season, with current general trends pointing upward. Expected to handily exceed the 12-month average (0.72).
2) 2025-04-15: Also in the peak dry season. Confidence remains high but decreases slightly with horizon (0.66).
3) 2025-06-14: Transition into the wet season. Secularly rising violence might offset the seasonal dip somewhat, keeping it marginally above average (0.55).
4) 2025-09-12: Peak wet season, where violence historically reaches its annual minimum. Good chance of being below the 12-month average (0.44).
5) 2026-03-16: One year out. This is a return to peak dry season, but the 12-month forecast horizon introduces substantial uncertainty about long-term peace or escalation, pulling the probability closer to 0.5 (0.53).
